title: SKY VIEW OBSERVATORY - Updated June 2026 - Yelp
content: Great place! Great people Only about $25 to see great views. Rally close to the library. Had a great visit and they have a Piroshki place in the lobby!

title: Sky View Observatory at the Columbia Center Admission Tickets
content: Highly recommend visiting here. We had fantastic weather that day and the views from the top were stunning. No crowds. Elevator is spacious.

title: Sky View Observatory
content: #21 in Best Things To Do in Seattle. Billed as the tallest building in Seattle, the Sky View Observatory on the 73rd floor of the Columbia Center provides stunning panoramic views of Seattle, Puget Sound, Mount Rainier and the surrounding landscapes. To enjoy cocktails and light bites like flatbreads and wraps, head to the onsite Sky View Cafe & Bar. Recent travelers strongly recommend choosing a visit here over the Space Needle, thanks to more affordable admission and fewer crowds that result in little to no wait time. For the mesmerizing sight of an illuminated city, reviewers suggest visiting the observatory in the evening. See all Hotels in Seattle ». Though Pike Place Market is one of the most tourist-heavy attractions in Seattle (plan to run into crowds, especially on the weekends) that's no reason to scratch it off your to-do list. See full list of Best Things To Do in Seattle ».
